name: Catuaba
summary: Catuaba has limited preclinical CNS research and no robust human clinical evidence.
description: Catuaba is a botanical category with poorly standardized constituents; mechanistic mapping is limited, so evidence should be treated as heterogeneous.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.
contraindications: known hypersensitivity to catuaba bark or related tree bark
pregnancy or breastfeeding due to a rodent study showing impaired implantation and lack of human safety data
MAOI antidepressants or SSRIs or other serotonergic medications due to MAO-A inhibiting activity and a theoretical serotonin syndrome risk
hypertension or cardiovascular disease warrants clinician guidance due to stimulant-like nervous system effects
overall drug interaction profile is poorly studied so combining with other medications warrants clinician guidance
